sentence_reviews.text: Privacy and governance combine data rights, consent, access control, licensing, retention, audit, risk ownership, and regulatory obligations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF covers privacy, accountability, governance, documentation, risk ownership, and compliance-oriented risk management.
sentence_reviews.notes: Broadly supported, but licensing/retention specifics are synthesized governance terms.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Federated & privacy-preserving learning, Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Privacy, governance, licensing & audit is modeled as depending on Federated & privacy-preserving learning, Databases & information retrieval and feeding downstream nodes such as API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors, Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Content credentials, signing & provenance.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
